data.title: FibroBiologics clears Nasdaq compliance, meets $2.5M equity rule
data.description:
• FibroBiologics (FBLG), a clinical-stage biotech company, announced on Monday that it has regained full compliance with Nasdaq listing rules.
  • The company confirmed it now meets key requirements, including minimum $2.5M stockholders’ equity and $1.00 bid price.
  • Although the issues are resolved, the company will still be monitored for one year, and its stock will continue trading normally on Nasdaq.
  • FBLG is trading ~1.31 lower at $1.51 in after-hours trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tanker arrives at Golden Pass LNG terminal to load first export cargo
data.description:
A tanker has arrived at the Golden Pass ​facility in Texas to collect the plant's inaugural export ‌of liquefied natural gas, Golden Pass LNG said Monday, after finally beginning production earlier this year following prolonged delays in construction.

The Al Qaiyyah LNG tanker, which arrived ​at the facility on Monday, is owned by QatarEnergy, which has a 70% stake ⁠in the plant; Exxon Mobil (XOM) holds the other ​30% of the project's equity.

The Exxon-chartered (XOM) LNG carrier HL Sea Eagle ⁠was ​in the Gulf of Mexico on Monday, ​with LSEG ship-tracking data showing it headed to Golden Pass, a sign it could ​load the second shipment from the facility, Reuters reported.

Golden Pass is expected to be one of the largest U.S. LNG export plants when fully operational, with total capacity of more than 18M metric tons/year.

The project has faced delays ​and cost overruns since construction began in 2019, including the bankruptcy of its original lead contractor; Golden Pass said on March 30 that it had produced its first ​LNG.

data.title: AGNC Investment Q1 earnings hold up as book value declines amid MBS volatility
data.description:
AGNC Investment (AGNC) posted stronger-than-expected Q1 earnings on Monday even as its book value dipped due to volatility in the mortgage-backed securities market at the end of the quarter.

Q1 net spread and dollar roll income per share of $0.42, beating the average analyst estimate of $0.37, rose from $0.35 in Q4 2025 and declined from $0.44 in Q1 2025.

The current quarter excludes less than $0.01 per share of estimated “catch-up” premium amortization due to its change in projected constant prepayment rate (“CPR”) estimates.

Tangible net book value of $8.38 per common share at March 31, 2026, dropped from $8.88 at Dec. 31, 2025. Economic return on tangible common equity declined to -1.6% from 11.6% in the previous quarter. Q1 2026 economic return comprised $0.36 dividends per common share and a $0.50 decrease in tangible net book value per common share.

AGNC Investment (AGNC) stock slipped 0.2% in Monday after-hours trading.

"In January and February, the administration's focus on reducing interest rate volatility, maintaining mortgage spread stability, and improving housing affordability drove strong performance across the broader fixed income complex and Agency MBS specifically," said President and Chief Investment Officer Peter Federico.  "This favorable investment environment was, however, quickly eclipsed in March by the war in Iran and the potential for more widespread conflict in the Middle East."

The increase in volatility and negative shift in investor sentiment caused agency MBS spreads to benchmark rates to widen, leading to AGNC's negative economic return on tangible common equity in Q1, he added.

Q1 net interest income was $319M vs. $206M in Q4 and $159M in last year’s Q1.

Its investment portfolio totaled $94.7B as of March 31, 2026, vs. $94.8B as of Dec. 31, 2025.

The REIT’s annualized net interest spread, including the TBA position and interest rate swaps excluding “catch-up” premium amortization, was 2.06% vs. 1.81% in Q4.

Its fixed-rate agency mortgage-backed securities and TBA securities’ weighted average coupon was 4.95% at March 31, 2026, vs. 5.12% at Dec. 31.

AGNC’s (AGNC) weighted average actual CPR for Q1 was 13.2% vs. 9.7% in the prior quarter.

During the quarter, AGNC (AGNC) issued 38.0M common shares through its at-the-market offerings for net proceeds of $401M.

Conference call on April 21 at 8:30 AM ET.

data.title: 3 things to look out for on Tuesday
data.description:
Wall Street closed lower on Monday as optimism over a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal faded, with escalating tensions in the Middle East weighing on investor sentiment.

On the session, the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) lost -0.2%, the S&P 500 (SP500) closed in the red by  -0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) handed back -0.1%.

Now, here are three focus points for investors on Tuesday: 

  • Traders will also be closely watching the latest mortgage data, including applications, the refinance index, and average mortgage rates, for signals on housing market momentum. The release will be parsed for evidence of demand sensitivity to interest rates and shifts in refinancing activity, which could influence expectations for consumer strength and the broader economic outlook. 
  • Market participants will also be focused on a fresh round of defense and aerospace earnings, with RTX Corporation (RTX), GE Aerospace (GE), and Northrop Grumman (NOC) all set to report. Results will be closely analyzed for signals on defense spending trends, order backlogs, margin resilience, and supply chain stability, offering insight into the durability of the sector amid ongoing geopolitical tensions.
  •  Investors will further be watching earnings from Capital One Financial Corporation (COF), with a focus on trends across its credit card and consumer banking businesses. The report will be closely examined for signals on loan growth, credit quality, net interest margins, and consumer spending behavior, which could shape sentiment toward the broader financial sector.

data.title: NETSTREIT reports Q1 results
data.description:
• NETSTREIT press release (NTST): Q1 FFO of $0.32.
  • Completed $239.0 Million of Gross Investment Activity at 7.5% Blended Cash Yield for First Quarter.
  • Increases 2026 AFFO Per Share Guidance to a New Range of $1.36 to $1.39.
  •  $314.3 Million of Gross Forward Equity Sales via February Follow-On and ATM in First Quarter.
  • Increases 2026 Net Investment Guidance Range to $550 Million to $650 Million 

2026 GUIDANCE

  • The Company is increasing its full-year 2026 AFFO per share guidance range to $1.36 to $1.39 from $1.35 to $1.39 and increasing net investment activity guidance to $550.0 million to $650.0 million from $350.0 million to $450.0 million. The Company continues to expect cash G&A to range between $16.0 million and $17.0 million (exclusive of transaction costs and severance payments). In addition, the company's AFFO per share guidance range now includes $0.03 to $0.06 per share of estimated dilution (vs. the previous $0.015 to $0.03 per share) due to the impact of the company's outstanding forward equity calculated in accordance with the treasury stock method.

data.title: Swvl Holdings regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Swvl (SWVL) has regained compliance with Nasdaq listing rules on minimum equity and net income.
  • The company now meets $2.5M equity and $500K net income requirements.
  • Swvl reported $2.9M in stockholders’ equity and $1.3M in net income for FY2025.
  • The company had previously been out of compliance since October 31, 2025, including market value requirements.

data.title: Coinbase-Bybit eyes tokenized U.S. stocks as volume hits $2.87B
data.description:
Coinbase Global ($COIN) is reportedly collaborating with Bybit on a major push into asset tokenization, marking a potential step toward bringing traditional U.S. equities on-chain for global users. 

The discussions, which are still ongoing, focus on tokenized stocks, custody solutions, and distribution infrastructure for both U.S. public shares and pre-IPO assets.

As per CoinDesk's sources, the arrangement does not involve any equity stake acquisition or a formal deal enabling Bybit’s direct entry into the U.S. market. Instead, Bybit’s planned U.S. expansion is expected to run via a separate entity led by former co-CEO Helen Liu.

A local partner is expected to handle licensing and compliance, while Bybit may contribute technology, product infrastructure, and liquidity.

The latest report suggests that both companies were already discussing wider cooperation, mainly around regulated market access and tokenized asset distribution. 

This move comes as tokenized equities are growing fast, with trading volumes reaching about ~$2.87B in March, rising over 80% in just 30 days. At the same time, the total number of holders crossed 200K, reported RWA.xyz.

Industry analysts believe such partnerships could significantly expand global access to U.S. assets via a single digital platform. Meanwhile, Intercontinental Exchange ($ICE), owner of the NYSE, has also moved into the space by taking a stake in crypto exchange OKX (OKX-USD).

Following the news, $COIN rose ~3% to $211.68.

data.title: AXT plans public stock offering; shares down
data.description:
• AXT (AXTI) announced plans to offer shares of common stock in a public offering.
  • Underwriters will have a 30-day option to purchase up to 15% additional shares (overallotment option).
  • Proceeds will primarily support subsidiary Beijing Tongmei Xtal Technology in expanding indium phosphide substrate production.
  • Funds will also be used for R&D of new or improved products.
  • Additional uses include working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • AXTI shares fell 7% post-market.
 
 

Your Premium trial has ended

data.title: Broadcom shares slip after nine-session winning streak
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) shares snapped nine straight sessions of gains on Monday, as the stock was 1.7% lower at $399.46.

The semiconductor and infrastructure software solutions company gained nearly 22% in the preceding nine sessions. So far this year, the stock has added over 16%, compared to the 4% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index (SP500).

AVGO is up 24% over the past one month.

Most recently, Broadcom entered into a three-year partnership with Meta Platforms (META) to help the company develop its artificial intelligence chips and deploy them into data centers.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, AVGO has a Hold rating with a score of 3.5 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability and growth, while it received F in the valuation factor.

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

Turning to the Wall Street, 44 analysts have given the stock a Buy or above rating. Three gave the stock hold recommendation, while none gave Sell rating to AVGO.

 

“Despite a premium valuation, Broadcom offers significant upside as earnings scale rapidly, with potential for substantial re-rating as AI demand continues accelerating through 2027,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: CNB Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87, revenue of $83.32M
data.description:
• CNB Financial press release (CCNE): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87.
  • Revenue of $83.32M.
    • Loans - Excluding $78.3 million of syndicated loan balances, loans were $6.4 billion as of March 31, 2026. Organic loans decreased for the quarter by $67.3 million, or 1.41% (5.73% annualized), compared to December 31, 2025.1 The decrease in organic loans was driven primarily by an increased level of prepayments in certain larger Commercial Real Estate (“CRE”) loans.
    • Deposits - At March 31, 2026, total deposits were $7.1 billion. Including $89.9 million in deposits classified as held for sale, organic deposit growth for the quarter totaled $115.0 million, or 1.62% (6.55% annualized), compared to December 31, 2025.1
    • Net Interest Margin - Net interest margin was 3.83% for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared to 3.84% for the three months ended December 31, 2025. Net interest margin on a fully tax-equivalent basis, a non-GAAP measure, was 3.84% and 3.84%, for the three months ended March 31, 2026 and December 31, 2025, respectively.1 Included in net interest margin on a fully tax-equivalent basis was $3.0 million and $3.2 million of purchase accounting loan accretion for the three months ended March 31, 2026 and December 31, 2025, respectively.
    • Credit Quality - Total nonperforming assets were approximately $49.2 million, or 0.58% of total assets, as of March 31, 2026, compared to $42.2 million, or 0.50% of total assets, as of December 31, 2025.
